key_insight: The paper replaces slow per-instance stochastic optimization with a learned proxy that predicts near-optimal sequential decisions in a single forward pass while preserving feasibility through decoding.
engineering_relevance: This enables sub-second order-fulfillment decisions for real-time logistics, making contextual stochastic optimization practical in high-volume omnichannel operations.
method: Train a scenario-embedded neural network offline on solver-generated C-SAA labels.
Use a decoder to enforce feasibility at inference time.
Optimize with a composite loss: imitation, constraint-violation penalty, and self-supervised cost alignment.
evidence_signal: In a simulator calibrated from JD.com transactional records, the proxy was about 2800x faster than online C-SAA, reduced realized fulfillment cost by 3.3% versus C-SAA, and cut total cost by at least 10.7% versus baseline policies.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of solver-generated labels, simulator calibration, and whether the deployment distribution matches the training scenarios.
paper_title: Learning Optimization Proxies for Sequential Contextual Stochastic Programs: An Order Fulfillment Application
